DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
No appearance by plaintiff(s)/cross-defendants/demurring parties
Attorney Ike M. Kaludi, Jr. (510-559-0320), specially appearing counsel for defendants/cross-complainants Boykin and Quizmundo
Via Court Call: Attorney Chad Wilson of Koenig Jacobsen LLP (949-756-0700), counsel for cross-defendant/demurring party Century 21

The Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANTs DIANA SAM and MYRON MOSKOVITZ's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained with leave amend for Plaintiff to separately allege (1) a cause of action for breach of contract and (2) a cause of action for breach of covenant of good faith a fair dealing, specifying damages caused by each breach. Demurrer to the sixth, seventh and eight causes of action for fraud is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-complaint failed to allege damages element of this cause of action after a leave to amend to do so was granted. Demurrer to the elder abuse cause of action is overruled. Moving party does not cite any authority for the proposition that CC 3343 requirements are applicable to the elder abuse claim. Elder abuse claim is sufficiently alleged under Bounds v. Superior Court (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 468. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT

MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
